steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left of the blank is a complete independent clause about what astronomers detected: “Canadian astronomers detected a striking and unusual phenomenon occurring in the constellation Lepus, 160 million light-years from Earth.” On the right is a noun phrase that identifies what the phenomenon is: “the explosion of a massive star, also known as a supernova, an extremely bright yet transient event...”

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon is used to introduce a noun phrase that explains or identifies a preceding noun. The colon correctly introduces “the explosion of a massive star” to clarify what the phenomenon is.

Choices A, B, and
  • D all fail: A creates a sentence fragment
  • B creates a comma splice
  • D incorrectly uses a semicolon (semicolons join independent clauses
, not a clause and noun phrase).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
